sanskrit:
यज्ञशिष्टाशिनः सन्तो मुच्यन्ते सर्वकिल्बिषैः।
भुञ्जते ते त्वघं पापा ये पचन्त्यात्मकारणात्॥
transliteration:
yajña-śiṣṭāśinaḥ santo mucyante sarva-kilbiṣaiḥ |
bhuñjate te tv aghaṁ pāpā ye pacanty ātmakāraṇāt ||
literal_meaning: Those who eat the remnants of sacrifice are freed from all sins; but the wicked who cook only for themselves eat sin.
contextual_meaning: Actions performed with an offering-attitude integrate the psyche and dissolve guilt; self-referential actions reinforce the guilt-loop.
trigger_scenario: Eating lunch at desk while doom-scrolling vs. pausing to thank the cook, the farmer, and the paycheck that bought it.
anchor_text: Share the fruit of your labor; selfish gain alone feeds guilt.
